commit_scope: Move BML file scanning onto a generic native source scanner fed by Form-defined language lexicon data.

local_validation.notes: The generic scanner boundary proof returns 511 across Go/Rust/TypeScript kernels. Existing .bml file forward/backward and char escape proofs remain green at 131071 and 2147483647. The kernel-native audit now requires source_scan_file and fails if BML scanner tables or bml_scan_file re-enter kernels.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: BML source file scanning remains native-fast while language-specific scanner definitions live in Form/BML lexicon data instead of Go/Rust/TypeScript kernels.
test_flows: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk form-stdlib/tests/bml-generic-source-scanner-boundary-proof.fk

phase_gate.reason: This closes the scanner-boundary leak by removing BML scanner tables from primitive kernels. Full BML thesis parity remains open: complete grammar-body semantic lowering, generic expression/member/index/call lowering, statement coverage, and whole companion-corpus native execution are still required.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk defines bml-source-lexicon and calls source_scan_file with Form-owned BML scanner data.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-generic-source-scanner-boundary-proof.fk proves the same file scans differently under an alternate lexicon, showing native scanner behavior is data-driven.
scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py now fails if bml_scan_file or hard-coded BML scanner tables re-enter Go/Rust/TypeScript kernels.
